product.name: ArWoWear T1 Flame Arc Pro
product.familyDescriptorPl: Trudnopalne z ochroną przed łukiem elektrycznym
product.categorySourceGroup: Flame Retardant, Antistatic, Vat Dyed, Workwear Fabrics Resistant up to 100 Wash
product.titleDescriptor: 99% Cotton 1% Carbon Twill, Flame Retardant Antistatic Fabric, 220 gr/m'2
product.specLine: 220 g/m², 99% Cotton 1% Carbon, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), Antistatic, 2/1S Twill Fabric
descriptions: ArWoWear T1 Flame Arc Pro is light weight 220 gr/m', 99% cotton 1% Carbon, vat dyed, flame retardant, antistatic workwear fabric.
It has flame retardant function with EN ISO 11612 certification.
Due to carbon fibers used on the back side of the fabric it has antistatic certification. (EN 1149-5)
It is durable up to 100 washing.
99% Cotton 1% Carbon Twill, Flame Retardant Antistatic Fabric, 220 gr/m'2
220 g/m², 99% Cotton 1% Carbon, VAT Dyed, Flame Retardant (100 Wash Resistant), Antistatic, 2/1S Twill Fabric
technicalParameters: Tensile Strength | Warp>800N; Weft>600N | EN ISO 13934-1
Tear Strength | Warp>20N; Weft>15N | EN ISO 13937-2
Dimensional Change | -3% < Length < +3% ; -3% < Width < +3% | EN ISO 5077
Dry Cleaning Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 D01
Hot Press Fastness | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 X11
Perspiration Light Fast. | 4-5 | EN ISO 105 B07
Azo Test | No Azo Colorants. | EN ISO 14362-1
Flame and Heat Protection | Yes | EN ISO 11612

possibleButNotAccepted.reason: Fabric offers antistatic (EN 1149-5) and flame retardant (EN ISO 11612) protection, which are relevant properties for electrical/arc hazard environments, but no explicit statement ties it to the energy sector, electrical utilities or high-voltage work.
FR cotton with antistatic properties could suit heat/flame hazard environments like welding, but no explicit mention of welding, foundry or metalworking use is present.

stage1.noMatchReason: Product is explicitly described as a flame retardant, antistatic workwear fabric (application listed as 'Flame Retardant Shirt') certified to EN ISO 11612 and EN 1149-5, intended for general FR/antistatic workwear use. No explicit sector such as energy, welding, firefighting or military is stated in the supplied data.

evidence: Flame retardant, antistatic workwear fabric with EN ISO 11612 and EN 1149-5 certification
primary.rationale: The product is explicitly described as a general flame retardant, antistatic workwear fabric intended for shirts, with no narrower named sector (energy, welding, firefighting, military) explicitly stated. This fits the general industrial protective workwear category best.

secondary.rationale: No independent explicit secondary end use is stated; energy and welding are only advisory possibilities without direct textual support.

alternativesConsidered.reasonNotSelected: Antistatic (EN 1149-5) property alone does not establish explicit energy-sector use per rule 8 and application exclude notes.
FR property alone does not establish welding/metallurgy use; no explicit mention of welding or foundry.
EN ISO 11612 is a heat/flame protection standard but not specific to firefighter turnout gear; no explicit firefighting context.
